name: Bitcoin
description: The original cryptocurrency and digital store of value. Fixed supply of 21M coins with halving every 4 years reducing new issuance.
bullCase: Institutional adoption via ETFs bringing trillions in new capital. Digital gold narrative strengthens as sovereign debt spirals. Halving supply shock meets unprecedented demand.
bearCase: Regulatory crackdowns, quantum computing threats, or a global risk-off event driving correlation to equities. Energy consumption narrative resurfaces.
strategy: Core position (30-50% of crypto portfolio). DCA on dips below 200-day SMA. Trim above 2x from cycle low.
risks: Correlation to risk assets in crisis

name: Ethereum
description: The leading smart contract platform powering DeFi, NFTs, and L2 scaling. Transitioned to Proof of Stake with EIP-1559 burn mechanism making ETH potentially deflationary.
bullCase: ETH ETF approval drives institutional demand. L2 ecosystem explodes bringing billions of users. Deflationary supply + staking yield = triple-point asset. Real-world asset tokenization on Ethereum.
bearCase: L2 fragmentation cannibalizes L1 fees. Solana and competitors eat market share. Regulatory classification as a security.
strategy: Core position (15-25%). Stake for ~3-4% yield. Buy ETH/BTC ratio dips below 0.04.

name: Solana
description: High-performance L1 blockchain with 400ms block times and sub-cent transactions. Dominant in retail DeFi, memecoins, and consumer crypto apps.
bullCase: Firedancer client doubles throughput. SOL ETF approval. Dominant consumer chain for payments and DePIN. Network effects compound.
bearCase: Network outages erode trust. Memecoin narrative fades. FTX estate token sales pressure. Validator centralization.
strategy: Growth allocation (10-15%). Take profits above $300. Add on network upgrade catalysts.

name: BNB
description: Native token of Binance ecosystem including the BNB Chain (BSC). Quarterly burn mechanism reduces supply. Powers the world's largest crypto exchange.
bullCase: Binance regulatory clarity. BNB Chain captures emerging market users. Exchange dominance translates to token value accrual.
bearCase: Regulatory actions against Binance. CZ's legal issues. DEX migration away from centralized exchange tokens.
strategy: Medium allocation (5-10%). Hedge for exchange exposure. Benefits from broad market activity.

name: XRP
description: Digital payment protocol for cross-border settlements. Ripple's XRPL processes transactions in 3-5 seconds. Major banking partnerships worldwide.
bullCase: SEC case resolution brings clarity. SWIFT replacement narrative. CBDCs built on XRPL. Institutional payment corridors expand.
bearCase: Banks build own solutions. Competition from stablecoins for payments. Centralization concerns with Ripple Labs holding large supply.
strategy: Speculative allocation (5-8%). Event-driven around regulatory milestones.

name: Polygon
description: Ethereum L2 scaling ecosystem with zkEVM, PoS chain, and CDK for custom chains. Rebranded from MATIC to POL as the universal gas and staking token.
bullCase: zkEVM matures and captures enterprise adoption. AggLayer connects all Polygon chains. Major brand partnerships (Disney, Starbucks, Nike) bring mainstream users.
bearCase: Competition from Arbitrum and Optimism. Migration costs from PoS to zkEVM. Token migration confusion.
strategy: Growth allocation (5-10%). High beta play on L2 adoption thesis.
risks: Ethereum L1 scaling reducing L2 need

name: Arbitrum
description: Leading Ethereum L2 by TVL using optimistic rollups. Arbitrum One for DeFi and Orbit for custom L3 chains. Dominant DeFi ecosystem.
bullCase: Highest TVL of any L2. Orbit chain adoption creates demand for ARB. DeFi protocols increasingly choose Arbitrum as home base.
bearCase: Token has limited value accrual. DAO governance inefficiency. Competition from ZK rollups long-term.
strategy: Speculative (5%). High conviction L2 play if DeFi thesis holds.

name: Optimism
description: Ethereum L2 with the OP Stack powering Superchain - a network of interoperable L2s. Base (Coinbase), Worldcoin, and others built on OP Stack.
bullCase: Superchain network effects as more chains deploy on OP Stack. Revenue from sequencer fees across all OP chains. Coinbase's Base drives massive adoption.
bearCase: OP Stack chains may not share sufficient revenue back. Fault proof delays. Token inflation from grants.
strategy: Speculative (3-5%). Bet on Superchain thesis and Base growth.
risks: Revenue leakage to OP Stack chains

name: Render Network
description: Decentralized GPU rendering network connecting artists needing rendering power with GPU owners. Key infrastructure for AI, 3D rendering, and spatial computing.
bullCase: AI compute demand explodes. Apple Vision Pro and spatial computing drive 3D rendering demand. Decentralized GPU network cheaper than cloud providers.
bearCase: Centralized GPU providers (AWS, Azure) offer better reliability. Token economics unclear for GPU providers. Narrow use case.
strategy: Speculative AI play (5-8%). High conviction on AI compute narrative.

name: Fetch.ai (ASI)
description: AI agent infrastructure platform merged with Ocean Protocol and SingularityNET into the ASI Alliance. Autonomous AI agents for DeFi, mobility, and supply chain.
bullCase: ASI Alliance creates dominant AI-crypto ecosystem. Autonomous agents become the primary DeFi interface. Enterprise AI agent adoption grows.
bearCase: AI agent hype doesn't translate to real usage. Merger integration challenges. Competition from centralized AI companies.
strategy: Speculative AI play (3-5%). Pure narrative bet on AI agents in crypto.

name: Bittensor
description: Decentralized AI network where subnets compete to produce the best ML models. Miners contribute AI compute and are rewarded with TAO. The 'Bitcoin of AI.'
bullCase: Becomes the decentralized alternative to OpenAI/Google AI. Subnet ecosystem explodes with specialized AI models. Deflationary tokenomics drive scarcity.
bearCase: Subnet quality inconsistent. Centralized AI far ahead in capability. Complex system hard to understand for mainstream adoption.
strategy: High-risk speculative (2-3%). The upside is massive if decentralized AI thesis plays out.

name: Akash Network
description: Decentralized cloud compute marketplace. GPU and CPU providers lease capacity at 50-85% below AWS/Azure/GCP prices. Growing AI workload deployment.
bullCase: Cloud compute costs keep rising. Akash provides the cheapest GPU access for AI training/inference. Enterprise adoption accelerates.
bearCase: Reliability concerns vs centralized cloud. Limited GPU availability. Small team vs hyperscaler resources.
strategy: Speculative DePIN play (2-4%). Pure bet on decentralized compute.

name: Chainlink
description: Dominant oracle network providing real-world data to smart contracts. CCIP enables cross-chain communication. Essential infrastructure for all of DeFi.
bullCase: CCIP becomes the TCP/IP of blockchain interoperability. Staking goes live driving demand. RWA tokenization requires Chainlink data feeds.
bearCase: Token value accrual still unclear despite protocol dominance. Staking rewards may not justify price. Competition from API3, Pyth.
strategy: Core DeFi position (5-10%). Infrastructure play that benefits from any DeFi growth.

name: Aave
description: Largest decentralized lending protocol by TVL. Deploy across 7+ chains. Flash loans, rate switching, and institutional-grade risk management.
bullCase: GHO stablecoin gains traction. Real yield from protocol fees. Institutional DeFi adoption brings billions in deposits. Fee switch activated.
bearCase: Smart contract risk. Regulatory scrutiny on lending protocols. Competition from newer protocols offering higher yields.
strategy: Core DeFi (5-8%). Beneficiary of any lending/borrowing growth in crypto.

name: Uniswap
description: Largest decentralized exchange by volume. V4 introduces hooks for customizable pools. Uniswap X for intent-based trading with MEV protection.
bullCase: Fee switch sends protocol revenue to UNI holders. V4 hooks create an app store for AMMs. Uniswap X captures CEX volume migration.
bearCase: Fee switch may never activate due to regulatory concerns. DEX volume share shrinking to aggregators. Token has no value accrual.
strategy: Speculative DeFi (3-5%). Bet on fee switch and DEX volume growth.

name: Maker (Sky)
description: Protocol behind DAI, the largest decentralized stablecoin. Earns real yield from RWA (US Treasuries) and crypto-collateralized lending. Rebranding to Sky.
bullCase: RWA integration brings real yield. DAI/USDS becomes the dominant decentralized stablecoin. Protocol revenue used for MKR buybacks and burns.
bearCase: Regulatory risk around stablecoins. RWA exposure introduces TradFi risk. Rebranding confusion.
strategy: Value DeFi play (3-5%). Real revenue + buyback/burn model.
risks: DAI losing market share to USDC/USDT

name: Avalanche
description: High-performance L1 with subnet architecture for custom blockchains. Sub-second finality. Strong in gaming, institutional DeFi, and tokenized assets.
bullCase: Subnet adoption for institutional and gaming use cases. Partnership with major financial institutions. Evergreen subnets for compliant DeFi.
bearCase: Subnet adoption slower than expected. Competition from Cosmos and Polygon CDK. Token inflation concerns.
strategy: Growth L1 allocation (5%). Bet on institutional and gaming subnet adoption.

name: Cardano
description: Research-driven L1 blockchain with formal verification and peer-reviewed development. Hydra L2 scaling. Strong community and governance model.
bullCase: Hydra scaling delivers high throughput. Midnight privacy sidechain attracts enterprise. Research-first approach eventually pays off.
bearCase: Slow development pace. Low DeFi adoption vs competitors. Academic approach misses market windows.
strategy: Conservative L1 position (3-5%). Stake for yield while waiting for ecosystem growth.
risks: Low DeFi TVL relative to market cap

name: NEAR Protocol
description: Sharded L1 with chain abstraction making crypto invisible to end users. Account aggregation across chains. Strong AI integration focus.
bullCase: Chain abstraction becomes the UX standard. NEAR AI assistant captures mainstream users. Nightshade sharding delivers unlimited scalability.
bearCase: Chain abstraction may not matter to users. Low DeFi adoption currently. Team token unlocks create sell pressure.
strategy: Speculative L1 (3-5%). Bet on chain abstraction and AI integration narrative.
risks: Competition from account abstraction on other chains

name: Kaspa
description: Fastest Proof-of-Work blockchain using GHOSTDAG protocol for parallel block processing. 1-second block times. Fair launch with no premine.
bullCase: Only fair-launch PoW coin gaining institutional interest post-BTC ETF. Smart contracts (Kasplex) enable DeFi. Mining ecosystem grows rapidly.
bearCase: No smart contracts yet limits utility. PoW energy concerns. Low liquidity and exchange listings.
strategy: High-risk speculative (2-3%). Pure PoW narrative play with massive upside if smart contracts deliver.

name: Litecoin
description: Silver to Bitcoin's gold. Faster block times (2.5 min) and lower fees. One of the oldest and most battle-tested blockchains. Wide merchant adoption.
bullCase: MWEB privacy features attract users. LTC ETF potential. Proven 13+ year track record. Low fees make it practical for payments.
bearCase: Limited innovation. No DeFi ecosystem. Lightning Network on BTC reduces LTC's speed advantage.
strategy: Conservative allocation (2-3%). Steady performer in bull markets with lower downside.

name: Dogecoin
description: The original memecoin. Elon Musk support, massive community, and increasing utility through payment integrations. Inflationary supply of 5B DOGE/year.
bullCase: X (Twitter) payment integration. DOGE ETF filing. Elon continues support. Largest memecoin community creates network effect.
bearCase: Inflationary supply. Meme narratives fade. Over-reliance on Elon Musk. No DeFi ecosystem.
strategy: Meme allocation (3-5%). Trade the Elon catalyst cycle. Set strict stop-losses.

name: Immutable
description: Leading blockchain gaming platform with zero gas fees for NFT minting/trading. Immutable zkEVM powered by Polygon. 200+ games in pipeline.
bullCase: Blockchain gaming hits mainstream. AAA game studios launch on Immutable. NFT trading volume surges. Passport wallet simplifies onboarding.
bearCase: Blockchain gaming has failed to attract mainstream gamers. Most game launches underwhelm. Token unlock pressure.
strategy: Speculative gaming play (2-4%). High risk but massive upside if gaming thesis plays out.

name: The Sandbox
description: Decentralized virtual world and gaming platform. User-created content with NFT land parcels. Major brand partnerships including Adidas, Warner Music, Gucci.
bullCase: Metaverse narrative returns. Brand partnerships drive real user engagement. Creator economy in virtual worlds grows.
bearCase: Metaverse hype has died. User retention is poor. Token inflation from rewards. Competition from centralized platforms.
strategy: Speculative metaverse play (1-3%). Only if metaverse narrative revives.

name: Pepe
description: Ethereum-based memecoin featuring the Pepe the Frog meme. No utility, pure community and speculation. Largest Ethereum memecoin by market cap.
bullCase: Meme supercycle driven by retail FOMO. Largest Ethereum memecoin has staying power. Community-driven listing pushes.
bearCase: Zero fundamental value. Liquidity dries up in bear markets. Infinite competition from new memecoins.
strategy: Pure speculation (1-2% max). Trade momentum only. Never hold through bear markets.

name: Bonk
description: Solana's largest memecoin. Community airdropped to Solana ecosystem participants. Integrated into Solana DeFi protocols and payment apps.
bullCase: Solana ecosystem growth lifts all SOL tokens. Memecoin integration into DeFi (BonkBot) creates utility. Community burns reduce supply.
bearCase: Pure memecoin with no value. SOL ecosystem dependency. Competition from newer Solana memes.
strategy: Pure speculation (1-2% max). Ride SOL ecosystem momentum.

name: Monero
description: The leading privacy cryptocurrency. Ring signatures, stealth addresses, and RingCT make transactions untraceable. Strongest privacy guarantees in crypto.
bullCase: Privacy becomes essential as surveillance increases. Atomic swaps with BTC. Growing adoption in regions with financial censorship. CBDC backlash.
bearCase: Exchange delistings continue. Regulatory pressure on privacy coins. Stigma from illicit use association.
strategy: Privacy hedge (2-3%). Not for everyone but provides unique portfolio diversification.
